synthesis.summary: Centene surged 13.4% on strong Medicaid enrollment results reported in earnings, which reversed a period of significant investor pessimism around the stock. The move's magnitude likely reflects short-covering and relief buying on top of the fundamental beat, as the 5-day gain of 26% and overbought RSI of 82.8 suggest more than just the earnings news is at play — ongoing weakness in commercial enrollment means the fundamental picture is mixed, not fully bullish.

ZBH dropped 11.25% on 3.6x normal volume, likely driven by a CFO departure announced today — executive exits at this level typically trigger sharp selloffs as investors reprice leadership risk. A tariff benefit mention in the same coverage provided insufficient offset. The RSI of 28 confirms the severity of the selloff, but the single medium-impact news item doesn't fully explain the magnitude.

ATEC dropped ~9% today on below-average volume (rel. volume 0.87) with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The RSI at 34.5 suggests the stock is approaching oversold territory, and the 5-day loss of ~10% indicates sustained selling pressure, but the catalyst is not identifiable from available information.
